phases.name: Globe Implementation
phases.description: Add 3D globe canvas element, styles, and animation script to index.html

tasks.title: Add #globe canvas element to index.html body
tasks.description: Insert <canvas id="globe"></canvas> in the body of index.html, between the .noise div and the .orb divs.
acceptance_criteria: Canvas is placed between .noise div and .orb divs

tasks.title: Add CSS styles for #globe canvas
tasks.description: Insert #globe CSS rule into the <style> block after the #particles rule. Set position:fixed, centered with transform, z-index:2, pointer-events:none, opacity:0.85.
acceptance_criteria: #globe CSS rule exists in style block
Globe is positioned fixed and centered

tasks.title: Add globe rendering JavaScript IIFE
tasks.description: Append a new IIFE <script> block before </body>. Implement: canvas sizing based on window dimensions (R = 38% of smaller dimension), ocean gradient sphere, lat/lon grid lines, simplified continent polygon outlines, atmosphere glow ring, specular highlight, shadow gradient, and requestAnimationFrame rotation loop. Use let (not const) for R, SIZE, cx, cy so resize handler can update them without location.reload().
acceptance_criteria: Globe renders on canvas with ocean gradient
Resize handler recalculates dimensions using mutable let vars (no location.reload())

tasks.title: Verify build and visual output in browser
tasks.description: Open index.html in a browser and confirm: globe appears centered, rotates, shows grid/continents/glow/highlight, countdown and text remain above globe, no JS console errors.
acceptance_criteria: Globe canvas visible and centered on page
Grid lines, continents, atmosphere glow, specular highlight all visible
Countdown and container text render above globe (z-index ordering correct)
